strategy_overview: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles is a small set (190 cards) with only five supported enemy-color archetypes: WB Sneak, RW Alliance, UR Artifacts, GU Mutagen, and BG Disappear. The format plays slower than expected — UR Artifacts and BG Disappear are grindy midrange decks, while only RW Alliance is truly aggressive. Blue and Green have the strongest commons; prioritize them early. Artifact synergies (UR) are the deepest and most powerful strategy. Mutagen tokens are key glue — they are artifacts for UR, trigger disappear for BG, and provide +1/+1 counters for GU. Food tokens in BG serve a similar multi-role purpose. There are common dual lands for all five enemy pairs, so mana is reasonable. Legendary commons are frequent — avoid drafting duplicate copies. A 5-color good stuff pile is viable if you pick up enough fixing (Everything Pizza, Frog Butler, dual lands). When in doubt, draft UR Artifacts — it has the best synergy depth and payoffs in the format.

WU.notes: Not a supported pair — no gold signposts. Can function as a go-wide or artifacts shell using shared blue artifact cards and white evasion. Playable but lacks focused payoffs.

WB.notes: Tempo deck using cheap evasive attackers to enable powerful Sneak cards. Needs a good mix of enablers (Dream Beavers, Featherbrained Filcher) and payoffs (Karai, Leonardo). Sneak cards are also castable at full cost.

WR.notes: Go-wide aggro that triggers Alliance by flooding the board with creatures. Blink effects (Go Ninja Go, The Neutrinos) re-trigger Alliance and ETB effects. Needs a lean curve — prioritize 2-drops. Weakest supported archetype but still functional.

WG.notes: Not a supported pair. Can draft a decent creature-based deck using green's strong commons and white's evasion, but no dedicated synergy payoffs.

UB.notes: Not supported but surprisingly functional. Blue provides artifact synergies while black provides removal and disappear enablers. Krang & Shredder is hybrid and fits here.

UR.notes: Best deck in the format. Deep artifact synergies snowball quickly — Baxter Stockman, Donatello variants, Brilliance Unleashed, and Krang are all bombs. Plays as a grindy midrange deck that builds incremental advantage. Mutagen tokens count as artifacts. Mouser cards are premium.

UG.notes: Counters-based midrange using Mutagen tokens to grow creatures. Synergies are shallow but card quality is high — blue and green have the best commons. Lessons from Life is a premium signpost. Good base for 5-color splashing.

BR.notes: Not supported but can combine red's artifact payoffs with black's removal. Lacks dedicated gold cards. Only draft if the seat is wide open.

BG.notes: Strong midrange deck that sacrifices Food and tokens to trigger Disappear payoffs. Pizza Face grows your board; Lord Dregg creates an insect army; West Wind Avatar draws cards. Food subtheme with Courier of Comestibles and Ragamuffin Raptor adds recursion. Can pivot into 5-color good stuff.

RG.notes: Not a supported pair. Red and green creatures are individually strong but lack shared synergies. Only viable if bombs force you here.

archetypes.name: UR Artifacts
archetypes.description: Tier 1 — the best deck in the format. Grindy artifact-based midrange that snowballs through Donatello variants buffing artifact creatures, Baxter Stockman granting +3/+0 and first strike, and Brilliance Unleashed providing removal-on-a-stick. Mouser tokens are the engine — cheap artifact creatures that grow with counters. Mutagen tokens from blue cards count as artifacts. Plays defensively early while building incremental board and card advantage, then slams expensive payoffs to close. Krang with affinity for artifacts is a game-ender. Don & Raph, Hard Science gives affinity to your next noncreature spell when you attack. Technodrome becomes a massive trampler once buffed.

archetypes.name: BG Disappear
archetypes.description: Tier 1.5 — strong midrange deck built around triggering Disappear by sacrificing Food tokens, Mutagen tokens, and trading creatures in combat. Pizza Face grows your entire board with +1/+1 counters; Lord Dregg creates an army of flying Insects; West Wind Avatar draws cards and is a huge body. Food subtheme adds consistency — Courier of Comestibles tutors Food, Ragamuffin Raptor recurs Food from graveyard, and Anchovy & Banana Pizza doubles as premium removal. Putrid Pals is a 5/5 for 4 with Disappear active. Can pivot into 5-color good stuff using green fixing and Everything Pizza.

archetypes.name: WB Sneak
archetypes.description: Tempo deck that uses cheap evasive creatures to enable powerful Sneak effects. Ideal enablers are 1-mana flyers (Dream Beavers, Featherbrained Filcher) that are cheap to replay after being bounced. Karai reanimates creatures on combat damage; Leonardo, Leader in Blue warps combat; Karai's Technique is a blowout mid-combat removal. Sneak cards are also castable at full cost, so they are never dead. Splinter, Hamato Yoshi rewards heavy Ninja density. Draft payoffs first since they are strong on their own, then fill in enablers.

archetypes.name: GU Mutagen
archetypes.description: Midrange +1/+1 counters deck using Mutagen tokens to incrementally grow creatures. Synergies are not deep — there are few cards that specifically reward hoarding Mutagen — but blue and green card quality is the highest in the set. Genghis Frog creates Mutagen when Mutants enter (but don't force Mutant tribal). Slithering Cryptid and Punk Frogs are solid curve plays. Lessons from Life is the premium signpost — ramp, counters, and card draw in one. Good shell for splashing bombs via Frog Butler and dual lands. Can lean into blue's artifact synergies or green's Disappear cards depending on what's open.

archetypes.name: RW Alliance
archetypes.description: The most aggressive archetype — go wide with cheap creatures and trigger Alliance for incremental damage and board buffs. The Neutrinos and Go Ninja Go provide unusual blink effects for Boros, re-triggering ETB abilities and Alliance. Mechanized Ninja Cavalry makes two bodies (two Alliance triggers) and two artifacts. Lita and Mighty Mutanimals reward creature density. Raphael, Tough Turtle pings on every creature entry. Keep the curve lean — prioritize 2-drops and only include the best 4+ mana cards. EPF Point Squad is a trap — looks like a payoff but is clunky. Prefer Wingnut and Lita as payoffs.

archetypes.name: 5-Color Good Stuff
archetypes.description: Fringe but viable strategy — pick up fixing aggressively (Everything Pizza, Frog Butler, dual lands, Escape Tunnel, Omni-Cheese Pizza) and play the best rares and removal from every color. Everything Pizza is the key uncommon — early fixing, late-game bomb. Ragamuffin Raptor recurs it from graveyard. GU or BG make the best base pairs. The Last Ronin is worth splashing for in any deck. Only attempt if you see heavy fixing flowing and premium bombs in multiple colors.
